en: TO THE CHOIRMASTER. A PSALM OF DAVID. Be not silent, O God of my praise!
zh: 我所讚美的 神啊！ 求你不要緘默無聲，

en: For wicked and deceitful mouths are opened against me, speaking against me with lying tongues.
zh: 因為邪惡的人的嘴和詭詐的人的口，已經張開攻擊我； 他們用虛謊的舌頭對我說話。

en: They encircle me with words of hate, and attack me without cause.
zh: 他們說毒恨的話圍攻我， 無緣無故攻擊我。

en: In return for my love they accuse me, but I give myself to prayer.
zh: 他們以敵對的態度來回報我的愛； 至於我，我只有禱告。

en: So they reward me evil for good, and hatred for my love.
zh: 他們對我以惡報善， 以憎恨回報我的愛。

en: Appoint a wicked man against him; let an accuser stand at his right hand.
zh: 求你派一個惡人對付他； 派一個對頭站在他的右邊。

en: When he is tried, let him come forth guilty; let his prayer be counted as sin!
zh: 他受審判的時候，願他被判為有罪； 願他的禱告成為罪。

en: May his days be few; may another take his office!
zh: 願他的年日短少， 願別人取代他的職分。

en: May his children be fatherless and his wife a widow!
zh: 願他的兒女成為孤兒； 願他的妻子成為寡婦。

en: May his children wander about and beg, seeking food far from the ruins they inhabit!
zh: 願他的兒女流離失所，到處求乞； 願他們從他們破毀的家中被趕逐出去。

en: May the creditor seize all that he has; may strangers plunder the fruits of his toil!
zh: 願債主奪去他一切所有的； 願外人都劫掠他勞碌得來的。

en: Let there be none to extend kindness to him, nor any to pity his fatherless children!
zh: 願無人對他施仁慈； 願無人恩待他的孤兒。

en: May his posterity be cut off; may his name be blotted out in the second generation!
zh: 願他的後人被除滅， 願他們的名字在下一代被塗抹。

en: May the iniquity of his fathers be remembered before the LORD, and let not the sin of his mother be blotted out!
zh: 願他列祖的罪孽被耶和華謹記； 願他母親的罪惡不被塗抹。

en: Let them be before the LORD continually, that he may cut off the memory of them from the earth!
zh: 願這些罪常在耶和華面前， 好使他們的名號從地上被除掉。

en: For he did not remember to show kindness, but pursued the poor and needy and the brokenhearted, to put them to death.
zh: 因為他從沒有想起要施慈愛， 只知道迫害困苦、貧窮和傷心的人， 要把他們置於死地。

en: He loved to curse; let curses come upon him! He did not delight in blessing; may it be far from him!
zh: 他愛咒詛，願咒詛臨到他； 他不喜愛祝福，願福樂遠離他。

en: He clothed himself with cursing as his coat; may it soak into his body like water, like oil into his bones!
zh: 他以咒詛當作衣服穿上， 咒詛就像水一般進入他的內臟， 像油一樣進入他的骨頭。

en: May it be like a garment that he wraps around him, like a belt that he puts on every day!
zh: 願這咒詛像衣服一般給他披上， 並當作他常常束上的腰帶。

en: May this be the reward of my accusers from the LORD, of those who speak evil against my life!
zh: 願敵對我和用惡言攻擊我的， 都從耶和華那裡得到這報應。

en: But you, O GOD my Lord, deal on my behalf for your name's sake; because your steadfast love is good, deliver me!
zh: 至於你，主耶和華啊！求你為你名的緣故恩待我； 按著你美好的慈愛拯救我。

en: For I am poor and needy, and my heart is stricken within me.
zh: 因為我困苦貧窮， 我的內心傷痛。

en: I am gone like a shadow at evening; I am shaken off like a locust.
zh: 我像日影傾斜，漸漸消逝， 又像蝗蟲被抖掉。

en: My knees are weak through fasting; my body has become gaunt, with no fat.
zh: 我因禁食，雙膝軟弱無力； 我的身體消瘦枯乾。

en: I am an object of scorn to my accusers; when they see me, they wag their heads.
zh: 我成了他們羞辱的對象； 他們一看見我，就搖頭。

en: Help me, O LORD my God! Save me according to your steadfast love!
zh: 耶和華我的 神啊！求你幫助我； 求你按著你的慈愛拯救我，

en: Let them know that this is your hand; you, O LORD, have done it!
zh: 好讓人知道這是出於你的手， 是你耶和華所作的事。

en: Let them curse, but you will bless! They arise and are put to shame, but your servant will be glad!
zh: 任憑他們咒詛，只願你賜福； 願起來攻擊我的都蒙羞， 你的僕人卻要歡喜。

en: May my accusers be clothed with dishonor; may they be wrapped in their own shame as in a cloak!
zh: 願敵對我的披戴羞辱； 願他們以自己的恥辱為外袍披在身上。

en: With my mouth I will give great thanks to the LORD; I will praise him in the midst of the throng.
zh: 我要用口極力稱謝耶和華； 我要在眾人中間讚美他。

en: For he stands at the right hand of the needy, to save him from those who condemn his soul to death.
zh: 因為他必站在貧窮人的右邊， 拯救他脫離定他死罪的人。
